# HED (Hierarchical Event Descriptors)

> HED (Hierarchical Event Descriptors) is an ecosystem with a standardized, versioned, machine-readable vocabulary (HED schema) 
> for describing what happened during an experiment (the experimental events) as well as other relevant metadata such as 
> participant state and phenotype. 
> The HED ecosystem includes annotation validators in Python and JavaScript, an online web service, 
> and a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server as well as a suite of tools to
> support analysis using HED annotations. HED annotation behavior and interpretation are governed by the HED specification.
> HED is widely used in the Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS) and is being extended to Neurodata Without Borders (NWB). 
> HED endorsed by INCF, BIDS, NWB, NEMAR, and OpenNeuro.

## Start here
- [What HED is](https://www.hedtags.org): the introduction and the roles it serves (experimenter, annotator, analyst, tool developer, schema builder)
- [HED Resources](https://www.hedtags.org/hed-resources): tutorials, guides, and worked examples
- [Schema browser](https://www.hedtags.org/hed-schema-browser): explore the HED vocabulary
- [Online tools](https://hedtools.org/hed): validate, assemble, and remodel in the browser (latest features: https://hedtools.org/hed_dev)
- [HED use](https://www.hedtags.org/hed-resources/HowCanYouUseHed.html)
- [HED semantices](https://www.hedtags.org/hed-resources/HedAnnotationSemantics.html)

## The standard
- [Specification](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-specification): the HED spec, versioned and citable
- [Standardized HED vocabularies (HED schemas)](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-schemas): the vocabulary in XML, MediaWiki, and TSV, versioned (for example 8.4.0)
- [HED in BIDS](https://www.hedtags.org/hed-resources/BidsAnnotationQuickstart.html): how HED annotates events in BIDS datasets

## Validate and build (tools)
- [Python tools](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-python): `pip install hedtools`, for validation and remodeling
- [JavaScript validator](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-javascript): `npm install hed-validator`
- [Web service](https://hedtools.org/hed): UI plus a JSON web service (docs: https://www.hedtags.org/hed-server) for validation and analysis
- [MCP server](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-mcp): validate HED strings, BIDS TSV files, and JSON sidecars from any MCP client

## Notes for agents
- HED is machine-checkable by design. Generate an annotation, then validate it, do not guess. Use Python, JavaScript, the web service, or the MCP server.
- The schema is versioned; target a specific version and check compatibility before validating.
